description: Open item editor via HTTP/SSE provider; harness PATCHes the item externally; assert title input updates without page reload (SSE-driven live refresh). Verify dirty-guard: editing the title first must suppress the auto-refresh.

notes: provider-fs must be running on port 7701 (default via ./scripts/services start).
The harness PATCHes item-fix-auth-bug via the sidecar REST API on the 'patch-item' exec step.
The PATCH triggers an SSE change event on the provider-http subscription, which
propagates through useProviderSubscribe → ItemEditorPage refetch (~100 ms debounce).
A restore PATCH is issued at the end to reset the title to its original value.

actions.description: Baseline: confirm item loads with its original title
Harness PATCHes item-fix-auth-bug externally via provider-fs REST API
Wait for the SSE-triggered refetch to propagate the new title into the input
Harness restores original title so subsequent runs start from a clean state
Reopen editor to test dirty-guard: edits made before SSE fires must not be overwritten
Fire another PATCH while the user has unsaved edits (dirty=true)

mutations.value: Body updated externally while title is dirty

actions.description: Dirty-guard: SSE event must NOT overwrite the user's in-progress title edit

assertions.description: Title input reflects the externally patched value without page reload (SSE-driven refetch via useProviderSubscribe)
When dirty=true, SSE-triggered refetches do not overwrite the user's in-progress edits
No uncaught errors during SSE-triggered live refresh or dirty-guard suppression
